tone_and_voice: National-scale and indicator-literate -- SDG Progress writing must explicitly link citizen-level findings to specific SDG targets and indicators, complementing (never replacing) administrative national indicator reporting. Written for national reporting and voluntary review audiences.

section_rules.executive_summary: Must state explicitly which SDG target(s) this citizen-level evidence is relevant to, using specific target numbers where the data genuinely supports the link -- never a vague 'this supports the SDGs' statement.
section_rules.discussion: Should explain how citizen-level ground-truth data complements administrative indicator reporting, and note explicitly that this is a complementary, not a substitute, data source for official national SDG reporting.
section_rules.methodology: State clearly this is citizen-perception data collected via the described channels, distinct from administrative or facility-level SDG indicator data.
section_rules.limitations: Must state plainly that this citizen feedback is not a substitute for official national statistics office SDG indicator reporting, and should be read as complementary ground-truth evidence.
section_rules.recommendations: Recommendations should include incorporating this citizen-level evidence into the next national SDG voluntary review cycle, and cross-referencing findings against administrative data where possible.
section_rules.policy_implications: Should reference the specific SDG target and, where relevant, the specific national policy or programme area the finding is relevant to -- avoid generic 'this matters for the SDGs' framing.
section_rules.lessons_learned: Reflect on the value of citizen-level feedback as a complement to administrative SDG indicator reporting, and any specific findings that citizen data captured that administrative data alone would likely have missed.
section_rules.conclusion: State clearly which SDG-relevant dimension shows the strongest citizen-validated progress and which remains a named, unresolved gap.
section_rules.annexes: Include a explicit mapping of each major finding to its most relevant SDG target number(s), to support direct use in national reporting documentation.
section_rules.evidence_style: Cite the specific SDG target number alongside every major finding where genuinely relevant, and explicitly flag findings that do NOT have a clear SDG target mapping rather than forcing an artificial link.

common_risks: forcing an SDG target link where the data does not genuinely support one
treating citizen perception data as equivalent to official administrative SDG indicator data
under-reporting secondary concerns (affordability, access) because a stronger primary finding (responsiveness) dominates the narrative
typical_recommendations: incorporate citizen-level findings into the next national SDG voluntary review
cross-reference citizen-reported gaps (e.g. infrastructure) against administrative completion data
establish this citizen feedback exercise as a recurring input to national SDG tracking
typical_policy_implications: national SDG target-specific policy and resource allocation
sector_knowledge.typical_donor_expectations: National and multilateral SDG reporting stakeholders expect citizen-level evidence to be explicitly and specifically mapped to SDG targets, with clear acknowledgment of where it complements rather than replaces official administrative statistics.

forbidden_behaviors: Never claim this citizen-level survey data constitutes official national SDG indicator reporting -- it is complementary ground-truth evidence only.
Never force an SDG target citation where the underlying finding does not genuinely and specifically relate to that target.
Never allow a strong primary finding (e.g. responsiveness) to overshadow or omit a weaker but still SDG-relevant secondary finding (e.g. affordability, access).
